name: Bascons and the Notre-dame-de-la-course-Landaise chapel – loop
name_original: Bascons et la chapelle Notre-dame-de-la-course-Landaise – boucle
region_intro: Hiking around Bascons, a commune in the Landes department of Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, primarily features a gently undulating terrain. The elevation in the region ranges from approximately 217 to 351 feet (66 to 107 meters), indicating a landscape suitable for casual walks rather than challenging mountain hikes. The area includes features such as the Longs stream and a network of paths, many of which are shared with cyclists, offering accessible routes through the local countryside. Best hiking trails around Bascons The most popular hiking route is Bascons and the Notre-dame-de-la-course-Landaise chapel – loop, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 16 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the local area and passes by the historic Notre-Dame-de-la-Course-Landaise chapel. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cycle path loop from Laglorieuse, a moderate 6.8 miles (11.0 km) path. This route follows well-maintained cycle paths, offering a smooth surface through the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Cycle path loop from Mont-de-Marsan, a 7.7 miles (12.4 km) trail leading through the flat terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 13 minutes. Hiking around Bascons is defined by gently undulating terrain, cycle paths, and local streams, providing accessible routes for various activity levels. The network offers options for easy strolls and moderate excursions. The routes in Bascons are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Bascons's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Bascons?
A: There are over 50 hiking trails around Bascons, offering a variety of options for different preferences. The majority of these, about 40 routes, are considered easy, with 12 moderate trails and one more challenging option.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Bascons?
A: The terrain around Bascons is generally gently undulating, with elevations ranging from approximately 66 to 107 meters (217–351 ft). This makes the area suitable for casual walks and moderate excursions rather than strenuous mountain hikes. Many routes utilize well-maintained cycle paths and follow local streams like the Longs stream.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers in Bascons?
A: Yes, Bascons is well-suited for beginners and casual walkers. Over 40 of the available routes are classified as easy, providing accessible paths through the local countryside. These routes often follow cycle paths, offering smooth surfaces.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Bascons?
A: Many of the hiking routes around Bascons are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995965738/bascons-and-the-notre-dame-de-la-course-landaise-chapel-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bascons and the Notre-Dame-de-la-Course-Landaise chapel – loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 12.6 km long, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26266729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cycle path loop from Laglorieuse&lt;/a&gt;, covering 11.0 km.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Bascons?
A: The hiking trails in Bascons are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the accessible terrain and well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the hiking trails?
A: While hiking around Bascons, you can encounter several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995965738/bascons-and-the-notre-dame-de-la-course-landaise-chapel-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bascons and the Notre-Dame-de-la-Course-Landaise chapel – loop&lt;/a&gt; passes by the historic Notre-Dame-de-la-Course-Landaise chapel. Other nearby attractions include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/263849" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont-de-Marsan&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5712471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ménasse Pond&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5119677" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Human Rights Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Bascons suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, the generally flat and gently undulating terrain, combined with a high number of easy routes, makes hiking in Bascons suitable for families with children. Many paths are shared with cyclists, indicating smooth surfaces that are comfortable for younger hikers.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Bascons?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on every trail are not detailed, the natural and open character of the paths around Bascons, including cycle paths and rural tracks, generally makes them suitable for walking with dogs. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and respect local signage.

Q: Are there any routes that follow waterways or offer views of natural features?
A: Yes, some routes in Bascons incorporate natural features. The region includes the Longs stream, and many paths follow local waterways. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26266898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bassin de la Ferme de Beillet – Espace Vert de la Fougeraie loop from Stade André-et-Guy-Boniface&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the Bassin de la Ferme de Beillet.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Bascons?
A: Given the relatively mild climate of southwestern France and the gentle terrain, hiking in Bascons is enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable. Winter hikes are also possible, as the terrain is not challenging.

Q: Are there less crowded hiking trails available in Bascons?
A: With over 50 routes available, it's possible to find less crowded trails, especially outside of peak tourist seasons or by exploring some of the lesser-known paths. The region is not primarily known as a major hiking destination, which can contribute to a more tranquil experience on many routes.
tips: The Notre-Dame-de-la-Course-Landaise chapel dates from the 15th century and is built in the Romanesque style it is today, entirely dedicated to the memory and commemoration of the local Course Landaise which took place in the nearby arenas.

waypoints.name: Chapel of Our Lady of the Course Landaise
